summary.intent: Refactor Gossamer from an Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension to reduce install friction (auto-detect .entire/settings.json instead of manual repo registration) and improve context-switching between editing code and viewing Claude Code sessions.
summary.outcome: A fully functional VS Code extension was built with sessions list, session detail view with images and markdown, native checkpoint tree sidebar with diff support, embedded MCP server at /mcp, QuickPick search with FTS, VSIX packaging script, agent-aware labels/commands, onboarding flow with Entire CLI detection, and checkpoint remote configuration step.

repo: Each repo in ~/.gossamer/config.json has its own SQLite DB at ~/.gossamer/repos/<name>.db; the server is per-workspace, not global
Custom titles from /rename are stored as 'custom-title' events in ~/.claude/projects/.../*.jsonl, NOT in shadow or checkpoint branches — slug on LogEvent never changes
Images are stored as base64 in full.jsonl files on the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch; imageData and imageMediaType must be explicitly extracted during indexing
The .gossamer/checkpoints/ directory inside repos is a git worktree created by ensureWorktree() — intentional but pollutes git status; add to .gitignore

Session checkpoints on shadow branch represent what the agent actually did; Entire continues tagging new commits to the same session ID after the agent stops — use updatedAt+2min cutoff for Session view
The gossamer-testing worktree has its own file copies that must be synced manually from the main repo and rebuilt independently
Entire.io stores agent name in CheckpointSessionMetadata.agent; color selection is in-memory only and not persisted anywhere
The server must run on system Node.js (not VS Code's bundled Electron Node.js) due to better-sqlite3 native module version mismatch (NODE_MODULE_VERSION 140 vs 141)

code.finding: Originally listed only extension.ts and GossamerPanel.ts in 'include', causing SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts to never compile — fix by using 'src/**/*.ts' with webview excluded

Server is a static singleton (static server, static serverPort) that survives panel disposal; use process.kill(0) on child process to check liveness; getSystemNode() uses 'which node' not process.execPath
acquireVsCodeApi() can only be called once — store as window.__vscodeApi in the HTML page script, then vscodeApi.ts checks for it first before calling acquireVsCodeApi()
pushSchema() originally used Prisma CLI (PRISMA_BIN pointing to node_modules/.bin/prisma) which doesn't exist in bundled VSIX; replaced with schema.sql applied via raw SQL
startServer() calls syncLogContentFts() which queries LogContent table — on fresh DB with no schema, this throws 'no such table: LogContent' crashing the server before ensureRepoRegistered() runs
SQL parsing bug: splitting on ';' leaves lines starting with '-- CreateTable' comments; filter must strip comments per-line within each statement, not discard entire statements starting with '--'

initialLoadDone ref required to distinguish initial render from live updates for auto-scroll; scroll position check (scrollHeight - scrollTop - clientHeight < 80) must be inline at effect time, not in a separate scroll listener on Mantine ScrollArea viewport
panel.onDidChangeViewState must re-send server_ready when panel becomes visible — webview reloads when moved to new window and waits for the message again

workflow: VS Code webview panels reload when dragged to a new window — any state-dependent initialization (server_ready signals, acquireVsCodeApi) must be re-sent on onDidChangeViewState
Vite ESM output requires type='module' on the script tag in webview HTML; VS Code CSP needs explicit img-src data: for base64 images and connect-src for the server port

esbuild can bundle all pure-JS deps for VSIX; native .node files (better-sqlite3, node-pty plus their loaders bindings/file-uri-to-path/node-addon-api) must be copied separately — Prisma engines (62MB) are too large to bundle
Generate schema.sql via 'prisma migrate diff --from-empty --to-schema ... --script' and commit it; apply with raw SQL at startup instead of invoking Prisma CLI — safe to re-apply with IF NOT EXISTS
For VS Code TreeDataProvider: resourceUri must use absolute paths (join repoPath + relativePath) for SCM decorations to work; ViewColumn.Beside opens to the right of currently active panel
Chord keybindings (Ctrl+K G) are the safest extension convention — avoid Cmd+Shift+letter which conflicts with VS Code core
The gossamer-testing worktree pattern requires: edit in main repo, cp changed files to worktree, rebuild there — the workflow is fragile; consider a watch+copy script

friction: gossamer-testing worktree required constant manual file syncing and independent rebuilds — every code change needed cp + npm run build in the worktree
Server kept running stale compiled code; required explicit restart via new 'Gossamer: Restart Server' command; not discoverable
tsconfig.extension.json only including 2 files caused silent compilation failures — no error, just missing .js files at runtime
Schema SQL parsing bug (-- comments causing all statements to be discarded) caused 'no such table' errors on fresh install that were hard to diagnose
Prisma CLI too large (62MB with engines) to bundle in VSIX; required replacing runtime prisma db push with pre-generated schema.sql
VS Code's process.execPath points to Electron's node, not system node — better-sqlite3 version mismatch was the root cause of blank webview with no obvious error

Multiple rounds of 'images not rendering' debugging before tracing to: (1) webview CSP missing img-src data:, (2) imageData column not in schema, (3) Prisma client not regenerated, (4) server running stale code from gossamer-testing
npm workspace name 'gossamer-vscode' vs '-w packages/vscode' caused confusing build errors
acquireVsCodeApi() double-call crashed the webview silently — no error message, just a broken minimize button
open_items: Toggle panel keybinding (Ctrl+K G chord) does not work when the webview has keyboard focus — needs investigation or alternative approach
Future schema column additions need explicit ALTER TABLE migrations; IF NOT EXISTS CREATE TABLE approach only handles fresh installs, not upgrades with new columns
gossamer.vsix removed from git but packages/vscode/bundled-server/serve.js is still tracked — should add both to .gitignore and note in README that these are generated artifacts
The gossamer-testing worktree sync workflow is error-prone; consider a watch script that auto-copies changed files from main repo to worktree on save
Session color data (VS Code-style colored sessions) not stored anywhere by Claude Code yet — feature not implementable until entire.io persists it
OnboardingPanel has an unused 'context' field stored on this — minor TS warning to clean up

The 'Rewind to Checkpoint' command uses process.kill(pid, 0) to detect running agents — only works for Claude Code sessions tracked in ~/.claude/sessions/*.json; other agents (Gemini, Codex) not detected
